Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/1/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 8 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. The question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is Compulsory.
  3. Section - A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section - B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section - C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question No. 14 is a case based problem with 2 subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A
Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.(i)

If {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} x}[\mathrm{~F}(x)]=\frac{\sec ^4 x}{\operatorname{cosec}^4 x}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{F}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, then find F(x).

 2

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.
2.

Let A and B be two events such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}. Find the value of P(B/A).

 2
3.

Two balls are drawn at random from a bag containing 2 red balls and 3 blue balls, without replacement. Let the variable X denotes the number of red balls. Find the probability distribution of X.

 2
4.

Find the values of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, for which the distance of point (2, 1, {tex}\lambda{/tex}) from plane 3x + 5y + 4z = 11 is {tex}2\sqrt{2}{/tex} units.

 2
5.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} = x - y.

 2
6.

In a parallelogram PQRS, {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PQ}}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PS}}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find {tex}|\overrightarrow{P R}|{/tex} and {tex}|\overrightarrow{Q S}|{/tex}.

 2

SECTION - B
Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x \cos ^2\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right){/tex} = y; given that when x = 1, y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}.

 3
8.(i)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} are four non-zero vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, then show that {tex}(\vec{a}-2 \vec{d}){/tex} is parallel to {tex}(2 \vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{d}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2 \hat { i } - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k }{/tex}and {tex}2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} Find the two unit vectors parallel to its diagonals. Using the diagonal vectors, find the area of the parallelogram.
9.(i)

Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9}d x{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate using properties: {tex}\int \limits_{-\pi}^\pi(3 \sin x-2)^2 \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.
10.

Find the points on the line {tex} \frac { x + 2 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 1 } { 2 } = \frac { z - 3 } { 2 }{/tex} at a distance of 5 units from the point  P(1,3,3).

 3

SECTION - C
Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Evaluate {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x.{/tex}

 4
12.

A man is known to speak truth 7 out of 10 times. He threw a pair of dice and reports that a doublet appeared. Find the probability that it was actually a doublet.

 4
13.(i)

Using integration, find the area of the region {(x, y): 4x2 + 9y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 36, 2x + 3y {tex}\geq{/tex} 6}.

 4

OR
(ii)

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by lines x - y + 1 = 0, x = -2, x = 3 and x-axis.

CASE BASED / DATA BASED QUESTION
14.

Electrical transmission wires which are laid down in winters are stretched tightly to accommodate expansion in summers.

Two such wires lie along the following lines:
l1 : {tex}\frac{x+1}{3}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-3}{-2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+2}{-1}{/tex}
l2 : {tex}\frac{x}{-1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-7}{3}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+7}{-2}{/tex}

Based on the given information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Are the lines l1 and l2 coplanar? Justify your answer.

 2
(ii)

Find the point of intersection of the lines l1 and l2.

 2

Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/1/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 8 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minutes time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidate will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. The question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is Compulsory.
  3. Section - A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section - B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section - C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question No. 14 is a case based problem with 2 subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.(i)

If {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} x}[\mathrm{~F}(x)]=\frac{\sec ^4 x}{\operatorname{cosec}^4 x}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{F}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, then find F(x).

Solution:

F(x) = {tex}\int \tan ^4 x d x=\int \tan ^2 x \cdot\left(\sec ^2 x-1\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int\left(\tan ^2 x \sec ^2 x-\sec ^2 x+1\right) d x{/tex}
F(x) = {tex}\frac{\tan ^3 x}{3}-\tan x+x+C{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex}, F(x) = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex} gives C = {tex}\frac {2}{3}{/tex}
F(x) = {tex}\frac{\tan ^3 x}{3}-\tan x+x+\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac{\log x}{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} ...(i)
Taking {tex}I_1=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}.1dx{/tex}
Using integration by parts
{tex}I_1=\frac1{\left(\log x\right)^3}.x-\int\frac{\left(-3\right)}{x(\log x)^4}.xdx{/tex}
{tex}I_1=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx{/tex}
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}{/tex}

 2
2.

Let A and B be two events such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}. Find the value of P(B/A).

Solution:

∵ P(A) = {tex}\frac58{/tex}​​​​​​, P(B) = {tex}\frac12{/tex} , P(A/B) = {tex}\frac34{/tex}
∵ {tex}P\left(\frac{A}{B}\right)=\frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\frac34=\frac{P(A\cap B)}{\frac12}{/tex}​
​​​​​{tex}P(A\cap B)=\frac38{/tex}
Now, {tex}P\left(\frac{B}{A}\right)=\frac{P(B \cap A)}{P(A)}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}P\left(\frac{B}{A}\right)=\frac{\frac{3}{8}}{\frac{5}{8}}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}​​​​​​​

 2
3.

Two balls are drawn at random from a bag containing 2 red balls and 3 blue balls, without replacement. Let the variable X denotes the number of red balls. Find the probability distribution of X.

Solution:

Given, the number of red balls in the bag = 2
and, the number of blue balls in the bag = 3
Total number of balls in the bag = 2 + 3 = 5
Given, two balls are drawn at random without replacement.
X denotes the number of red balls and ‘X’ can be 0, 1 and 2.
When no red ball is drawn from the bag, X = 0,
P (X = 0) = {tex}\frac35\times\frac24=\frac3{10}{/tex}
When one red ball is drawn from the bag, X = 1 ,
P(X = 1) = P(RB) + P(BR) = {tex}\frac25\times\frac14+\frac35\times\frac24{/tex}
P(X = 1) = {tex}\frac2{20}+\frac6{20}=\frac8{20}=\frac25{/tex}​​​​​​​
When both balls are red X = 2,
P(X = 2) = {tex}\frac25\times\frac14=\frac1{10}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The probability distribution of X

X 0 1 2
f(X) {tex}\frac3{10}{/tex} {tex}\frac25{/tex} {tex}\frac1{10}{/tex}
2
4.

Find the values of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, for which the distance of point (2, 1, {tex}\lambda{/tex}) from plane 3x + 5y + 4z = 11 is {tex}2\sqrt{2}{/tex} units.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} given point is (2, 1, {tex}\lambda{/tex} ) and equation of plane is 3x + 5y + 4z = 11
Also distance between them is {tex}2\sqrt2{/tex} unit
distance {tex}=\left|\frac{Ax_1+By_1+Cz_1-d}{\sqrt{A^2+B^2+C^2}}\right|{/tex}
Here A = 3, B = 5, C = 4, D = 11, x1 = 2, y1 = 1, z1 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}
{tex}2\sqrt2=\left|\frac{3\times2+5\times1+4\times\lambda-11}{\sqrt{3^2+5^2+4^2}}\right|{/tex}
{tex}2\sqrt2=\left|\frac{6+5+4\lambda-11}{\sqrt{9+25+16}}\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}2\sqrt2=\left|\frac{4\lambda}{\sqrt{50}}\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}2 \sqrt{2} \times 5 \sqrt{2}{/tex} = 4{tex}\lambda{/tex}​​​​​​​
4{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 20​​​​​​​
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 5​​​​​​​

 2
5.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} = x - y.

Solution:

{tex}\log\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)=\left(x-y\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=e^{x-y}{/tex}​
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{e^x}{e^y}{/tex}
{tex}\int e^ydy=\int e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}e^y=e^x\;+C{/tex}
{tex}e^y-e^x=C{/tex}

 2
6.

In a parallelogram PQRS, {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PQ}}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PS}}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find {tex}|\overrightarrow{P R}|{/tex} and {tex}|\overrightarrow{Q S}|{/tex}.

Solution:

Let {tex}\vec{PQ}=\vec a=3\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat j+2\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\vec{PS}=\vec b=-\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat{\mathrm k}{/tex}

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, using the triangle law of vector addition
{tex}\vec a+\vec b=\vec{PR}{/tex} ...(1)
{tex}\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)+\left(-\hat i-2\hat k\right)=\vec{PR}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{PR}=2\hat i-2\hat j{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec{PR}\right|=\sqrt{4+4}=\sqrt8{/tex}
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQS,
{tex}\vec b-\vec a=\vec{QS}{/tex}
{tex}\left(-\hat i-2\hat j\right)-\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)=\vec{QS}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{QS}=-4\hat i-2\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec{QS}\right|=\sqrt{16+4}=\sqrt{20}{/tex}

 2

SECTION - B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x \cos ^2\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right){/tex} = y; given that when x = 1, y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right)=y{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}=y-x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx-\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex} ...(i)
It is a homogeneous differential equation
Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
Putting in eqn. (i)
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v-\cos^2v{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=-\cos^2v{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dv}{\cos^2v}=-\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int sec^2v\;dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
tan v = log x + C
tan v - log x = C
Put v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=C{/tex} ...(ii)
Putting x = 1 and y = {tex}\frac{tex}\pi{/tex}4{/tex}
{tex}\tan\left(\frac\pi4\right)+0=C{/tex}
C = 1
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=1{/tex}
This is the particular solution.

 3
8.(i)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} are four non-zero vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, then show that {tex}(\vec{a}-2 \vec{d}){/tex} is parallel to {tex}(2 \vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{d}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.

Solution:

Given, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}
To show: {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right)=0{/tex}
Taking LHS {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}
{tex}=(\overrightarrow a\times2\overrightarrow b)-\left(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow c\right)-4\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c{/tex}
​​​​​​{tex}=2(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b)-\left(4\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)-4\overrightarrow{(d}\times\overrightarrow b)+2(\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c){/tex} ​​​​​​​
{tex}=2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d)-4\left(\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)+4\overrightarrow{(b}\times\overrightarrow d)-2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d){/tex} ​​​​​​​= 0 = RHS

 3

OR
(ii)

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2 \hat { i } - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k }{/tex}and {tex}2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} Find the two unit vectors parallel to its diagonals. Using the diagonal vectors, find the area of the parallelogram.

Solution:

Let ABCD be the parallelogram.

According to the question,
{tex}\vec { A B } = 2 \hat { i} - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k } \text { and } \vec { A D } = 2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j} + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex}

 By using parallelogram law of addition , we get 
The diagonal {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { A B } + \vec { A D }{/tex}{tex}= \ 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex}
The diagonal {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { B C } + \vec { B A }{/tex}{tex}= \vec { A D } - \vec { A B } = 6 \hat { j } + 8 \hat { k }{/tex}
Now, the unit vector along {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { \vec { A C } } { | \vec { A C } | } = \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 16 + 4 + 4 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 4 \hat {i} - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 24 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { 2 \sqrt { 6 } } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } ( 2 \hat {i} - \hat { j } - \hat { k } ){/tex}
the unit vector along {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { \vec { B D } } { | \vec { B D } | } = \frac { 6 \hat {j } + 8 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 36 + 64 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 6 \hat {j} + 8 \hat { k } } { 10 } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 5 } ( 3 \hat { j} + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex}
Now, area of parallelogram ABCD
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } |{/tex}
Here, {tex}\vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 4 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } & { 8 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}= \hat { i } ( - 16 + 12 ) - \hat { j } ( 32 - 0 ) + \hat { k } ( 24 - 0 ){/tex}
{tex}= - 4 \hat { i } - 32 \hat { j} + 24 \hat { k }{/tex}

9.(i)

Evaluate {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9}d x{/tex}

Solution:

We have
I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9} d x+\frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} = I1 + I2 
Now I1 = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} 
Put t = x4 - 9 so that 4x3 dx = dt. Therefore
I1 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{4} \log |t|+\mathrm{C}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\mathrm{C}_{1}{/tex} 
Again, I2 = {tex}\int \frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} 
Put x2 = u so that 2x dx = du. Then
I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d u}{u^{2}-(3)^{2}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 6} \log \left|\frac{u-3}{u+3}\right|+\mathrm{C_2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C_{2}{/tex} 
Thus I = I1 + I2
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C{/tex}

Using Property
{tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=f\left(x\right){/tex}
{tex}=0\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=-f\left(x\right){/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} sin2x is even function, sin x is a odd function
So, {tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\sin xdx+4\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi dx-12\times0{/tex}
{tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\left[\frac12-\frac{\cos2x}2\right]dx+4\left[x\right]_{-\pi}^\pi{/tex}

10.

Find the points on the line {tex} \frac { x + 2 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 1 } { 2 } = \frac { z - 3 } { 2 }{/tex} at a distance of 5 units from the point  P(1,3,3).

Solution:

Given equation of line is:
{tex} \frac { x + 2 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 1 } { 2 } = \frac { z - 3 } { 2 } = \lambda ( \text { say } ){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { x + 2 } { 3 } = \lambda , \frac { y + 1 } { 2 } = \lambda , \frac { z - 3 } { 2 } = \lambda{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad x = 3 \lambda - 2 ,y = 2 \lambda - 1 , z = 2 \lambda + 3{/tex}
So, we have a point on the line is
{tex} Q ( 3 \lambda - 2,2 \lambda - 1,2 \lambda + 3 ){/tex}....(i)
Now, given that distance between two points
P(1, 3, 3) and{tex}Q ( 3 \lambda - 2,2 \lambda - 1,2 \lambda + 3 ){/tex}is
5 Units, i.e. PQ=5
{tex} \Rightarrow \sqrt {\left[ {{{(3\lambda - 2 - 1)}^2} + {{(2\lambda - 1 - 3)}^2} + {{(2\lambda + 3 - 3)}^2}} \right]} = 5{/tex}
{tex}\left[ {{\text{distance }} = \sqrt {{{\left( {{x_2} - {x_1}} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {{y_2} - {y_1}} \right)}^2} + {{({z_2} - {z_1})}^2}} } \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sqrt { ( 3 \lambda - 3 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 2 \lambda - 4 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 2 \lambda ) ^ { 2 } } = 5{/tex}
Therefore,on squaring both sides, we get,
{tex} ( 3 \lambda - 3 )^ 2 + ( 2 \lambda - 4 )^ 2 + ( 2 \lambda )^ 2 = 25{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 9 \lambda^ { 2 } + 9 - 18 \lambda + 4 \lambda ^ { 2 } + 16 - 16 \lambda + 4 \lambda ^{ 2 } = 25{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 17 \lambda ^ { 2 } - 34 \lambda = 0 \Rightarrow 17 \lambda ( \lambda - 2 ) = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \text { Either } 17 \lambda = 0 \text { or } \lambda - 2 = 0{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad \lambda = 0 \text { or } 2{/tex}
On putting {tex} \lambda{/tex} = 0 and {tex} \lambda{/tex} = 2 in Eq. (i). we get the required points as (-2,-1,3) or (4,3,7).

11.

Evaluate {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x.{/tex}

Solution:

Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i)
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii){tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex}
Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get ,
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) -  (0 - 1)]
{tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}

12.

A man is known to speak truth 7 out of 10 times. He threw a pair of dice and reports that a doublet appeared. Find the probability that it was actually a doublet.

Solution:

Let A be the event that man says a doublet appears.
E1 = Doublet appears, E2 = Doublet not appears
{tex}P\left(E_1\right)=\frac16,\;P\left(E_2\right)=1-\frac16=\frac56{/tex}
P(A/E1) is the probability when the man says it is a doublet and it is actually a doublet, P(A/E2) is probability when man says it is doublet but it was not
{tex}P\left(\frac A{E_1}\right)=\frac7{10},\;P\left(\frac A{E_2}\right)=\frac3{10}{/tex}
According to bayes' theorem,
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_1}A\right)=\frac{P\left(E_1\right)P\left({\frac A{E_1}}\right)}{P\left(E_1\right)P\left({\frac A{E_1}}\right)+P\left(E_2\right)P\left({\frac A{E_2}}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_1}A\right)=\frac{{\frac16}\times{\frac3{10}}}{{\frac16}\times{\frac3{10}}+{\frac56}\times{\frac3{10}}}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_1}A\right)=\frac{\frac1{20}}{\frac{11}{20}}=\frac1{11}{/tex}

Using integration, find the area of the region {(x, y): 4x2 + 9y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 36, 2x + 3y {tex}\geq{/tex} 6}.

Solution:

4x2 + 9y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 36
{tex}\frac{x^2}9+\frac{y^2}4\leq1{/tex} , {tex}\frac{x^2}9+\frac{y^2}4=1{/tex}
It is an equation of ellipse
And 2x + 3y {tex}\geq{/tex} 6 is an equation of line
So,

From the equation of ellipse {tex}y=\sqrt{\frac{36-4x^2}9}{/tex}
From equation of line = {tex}y=\frac{6-2x}3{/tex}
Area of shaded region = area under ellipse - area under line
Area = {tex}\int_0^3\left[\left(\sqrt{\frac{36-4x^2}9}\right)-\frac{6-2x}3\right]dx{/tex}
= {tex}\frac13\int_0^3\left[2\sqrt{9-x^2}-\left(6-2x\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac13\left[2\times\left\{{\textstyle\frac12}x\sqrt{9-x^2}+{\textstyle\frac12}\times9\sin^{-1}\left({\textstyle\frac x3}\right)\right\}-\left(6x-\left(\frac{2x^2}2\right)\right)\right]_0^3{/tex}
{tex}=\frac13\left[x\sqrt{9-x^2}+9\sin^{-1}\left(\frac x3\right)-6x+x^2\right]_0^3{/tex}
{tex}=\frac13\left[0+9\sin^{-1}\left(1\right)-18+9+0\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac13\left[9\frac\pi2-9\right]=\left(\frac{3\pi}2-3\;\right){/tex} sq. unit
